predictions.rationale: Market makes France overwhelming favourites (~76%) and their attack is the tournament's most prolific, with Mbappe and Dembele on four goals each. Sweden lose first-choice centre-back Isak Hien for the rest of the tournament (hamstring), gutting an already leaky defence that hasn't kept a clean sheet in a year. France win and should score freely.

Market shades Mexico as narrow favourites in a tight tie (43/33/24). Hosts kept three clean sheets in a perfect group with Jimenez rested and back, while Ecuador's miserly back line (Pacho, Hincapie) already stunned Germany 2-1. Home edge and altitude tilt it, but the strong tipster consensus is low-scoring with real draw/extra-time risk.
predictions.disagreement: Covers projects a 1-0 grind with extra-time risk; several experts lean Under and even a 90-min draw.

predictions.rationale: Market backs England strongly (~75%) and Declan Rice returns with Bellingham in form. But England are without Reece James and Quansah, and DR Congo — who held them 0-0 in the group and carry Wissa on the counter — will sit deep. England win against a low block, but a blowout is unlikely, so back a narrow result.

The market barely splits them (43/30/27). Senegal's likely loss of keeper Edouard Mendy (knee) behind an error-prone defence helps Belgium, but Lukaku is a fitness doubt and Senegal's athletic forwards (Mane, Sarr, Ndiaye) keep the upset live. Thin lean to Belgium's overall quality with De Bruyne pulling strings. Penalty risk.
predictions.disagreement: A Covers analyst leans Draw (+218) and Under 2.5, citing Belgium's flat group form and Lukaku's fitness.

predictions.rationale: Co-hosts are firm favourites and the Opta supercomputer agrees (USA 67.5%). Pulisic returns from a calf knock alongside in-form Balogun, while Bosnia have never kept a clean sheet in six World Cup matches. Crowd, depth and athleticism see the USA control and win.

Tournament favourites Spain are heavily backed (~72%) and conceded zero in the group. Nico Williams and Yeremy Pino are doubtful and Yamal is carrying a knock, but the depth still dwarfs an Austria side that shipped three to Algeria and lean on knocks of their own (Alaba, Arnautovic). Consensus is a controlled, win-to-nil Spain victory rather than a rout.

A veterans' duel with Portugal modest favourites (~54%) and a full, healthy squad under Martinez behind Ronaldo (two goals already). Croatia leaked four to England but recovered to beat Panama and Ghana, with Modric still orchestrating. Portugal's greater attacking depth edges a tight, experienced contest. Penalty risk.
predictions.disagreement: Market and tipsters split on goals — Squawka likes Over 2.5/BTTS while SportsLine and Racing Post lean Under, noting Croatia drag knockouts level.

predictions.rationale: Switzerland topped their group unbeaten but the line keeps drifting their way of caution (51 to 46 over two days). Subplot: Algeria's Petkovic faces the Swiss he once led, and Mahrez and Belghali can conjure moments. Swiss organisation should edge a tight one, but the market is increasingly wary. Penalty risk.

Market shades Egypt as narrow favourites in a near-even three-way (28/33/38). The big swing factor is Mohamed Salah, a genuine hamstring doubt facing a late fitness test, with Lasheen suspended and Fattouh injured thinning Egypt further. Australia are organised but lack a match-winner; a tentative lean to Egypt's edge in quality, but this could easily go the distance. Penalty risk.
predictions.disagreement: Lean is contingent on Salah passing his fitness test — if he sits, the draw (priced ~33%) becomes the live outcome.

predictions.rationale: Argentina are overwhelming favourites (~82%) against a Cape Verde side that managed only a 0-0 with Saudi Arabia. The gulf in quality from Messi's team is vast; expect a comfortable, controlled win.

Colombia were already firm favourites (~63%), unbeaten in the group and led by James Rodriguez and Luis Diaz. The key unpriced edge is Ghana's gutted attack: Kudus and Salisu are ruled out and Partey is carrying a groin issue, leaving Williams and Semenyo isolated. That pushes the lean past the market to a comfortable Colombia win.

Market makes Morocco clear favourites (~54%) and the world No. 7 are the strongest side Canada have faced, with Hakimi fit again after a hamstring scare. Canada lack the injured Alphonso Davies but earned a rest edge by winning their R32 in 90 minutes while Morocco needed 120-plus and penalties past the Netherlands. Lean Morocco, but the fresher hosts make this live. Penalty risk.
predictions.disagreement: Canada's rest advantage and home crowd give a contrarian angle on the underdog; Morocco have never lost to Canada.

predictions.rationale: Market makes Brazil favourites but only modestly (51/27/22). Vinicius is flying (four group goals) and Neymar is back on managed minutes, though Rodrygo and Estevao are injured, thinning the attack. Norway are a live underdog: Haaland has four goals in two starts and was rested in the dead-rubber loss to France, so he arrives fresh. Brazil edge a tight one. Penalty risk.
predictions.disagreement: Haaland's hot form makes Norway a genuine threat against a Brazil attack missing Rodrygo and Estevao.
